children.text: the encouragement of reform and improvement of, and innovation in, postsecondary education and the provision of educational opportunity for all students, including nontraditional students;

children.label: A
children.text: efforts that provide academic credit for programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: combinations of academic and experiential learning;

the establishment and continuation of institutions, programs, consortia, collaborations, and other joint efforts based on communications technology, including those efforts that utilize distance education and technological advancements to educate and train postsecondary students (including health professionals serving medically underserved populations);
the carrying out, in postsecondary educational institutions, of changes in internal structure and operations designed to clarify institutional priorities and purposes;
the design and introduction of cost-effective methods of instruction and operation;

the introduction of institutional reforms designed to expand individual opportunities for entering and reentering postsecondary institutions and pursuing programs of postsecondary study tailored to individual needs;
the introduction of reforms in graduate education, in the structure of academic professions, and in the recruitment and retention of faculties;
the creation of new institutions and programs for examining and awarding credentials to individuals, and the introduction of reforms in current institutional practices related thereto;

the introduction of reforms in remedial education, including English language instruction, to customize remedial courses to student goals and help students progress rapidly from remedial courses into core courses and through postsecondary program completion;

children.label: A
children.text: result in increased secondary school graduation rates of limited English proficient students; and

children.label: B
children.text: increase the number of participating late-entering limited English proficient students who pursue postsecondary education;

children.label: A
children.text: focus on poverty and human capability; and

children.label: i
children.text: a service-learning component; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the delivery of educational services through informational resource centers, summer institutes, midyear seminars, and other educational activities that stress the effects of poverty and how poverty can be alleviated through different career paths;

the provision of support and assistance for demonstration projects to provide comprehensive support services to ensure that homeless students, or students who were in foster care or were a ward of the court at any time before the age of 13, enroll and succeed in postsecondary education, including providing housing to such students during periods when housing at the institution of higher education is closed or generally unavailable to other students; and

the support of efforts to work with institutions of higher education, and nonprofit organizations, that seek to promote cultural diversity in the entertainment media industry, including through the training of students in production, marketing, and distribution of culturally relevant content.

children.heading: Planning grants
children.label: b Planning grants
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to make planning grants to institutions of higher education for the development and testing of innovative techniques in postsecondary education. Such grants shall not exceed $20,000.

children.heading: Center for best practices to support single parent students
children.label: c Center for best practices to support single parent students

children.heading: Program authorized
children.label: 1 Program authorized
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to award one grant or contract to an institution of higher education to enable such institution to establish and maintain a center to study and develop best practices for institutions of higher education to support single parents who are also students attending such institutions.

children.heading: Institution requirements
children.label: 2 Institution requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall award the grant or contract under this subsection to a four-year institution of higher education that has demonstrated expertise in the development of programs to assist single parents who are students at institutions of higher education, as shown by the institution’s development of a variety of targeted services to such students, including on-campus housing, child care, counseling, advising, internship opportunities, financial aid, and financial aid counseling and assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: assist institutions implementing innovative programs that support single parents pursuing higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: study and develop an evaluation protocol for such programs that includes quantitative and qualitative methodologies;

children.label: C
children.text: provide appropriate technical assistance regarding the replication, evaluation, and continuous improvement of such programs; and

children.label: D
children.text: develop and disseminate best practices for such programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No funds made available under this part shall be used to provide direct financial assistance in the form of grants or scholarships to students who do not meet the requirements of .section 1091(a) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prevent a student who does not meet the requirements of from participating in programs funded under this part.section 1091(a) of this title

children.heading: Priority
children.label: e Priority
children.text: In making grants under this part to any institution of higher education after , the Secretary may give priority to institutions that meet or exceed the most current version of ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1 (as such term is used in ) for any new facilities construction or major renovation of the institution after such date, except that this subsection shall not apply with respect to barns or greenhouses or similar structures owned by the institution.August 14, 2008section 6313(a)(6) of title 42

children.heading: Scholarship program for family members of veterans or members of the military
children.label: f Scholarship program for family members of veterans or members of the military

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: 1 Authorization
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into a contract with a nonprofit organization with demonstrated success in carrying out the activities described in this subsection to carry out a program to provide postsecondary education scholarships for eligible students.

children.label: I
children.text: serving on active duty during a war or other military operation or national emergency (as defined in ); orsection 1088 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: performing qualifying National Guard duty during a war or other military operation or national emergency (as defined in ); orsection 1088 of this title

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: died, or has been disabled, as a result of such service or performance; or

children.label: II

children.heading: Awarding of scholarships
children.label: 3 Awarding of scholarships
children.text: Scholarships awarded under this subsection shall be awarded based on need with priority given to eligible students who are eligible to receive Federal Pell Grants under subpart 1 of part A of subchapter IV.

children.heading: Maximum scholarship amount
children.label: 4 Maximum scholarship amount
children.text: The maximum scholarship amount awarded to an eligible student under this subsection for an award year shall be the lesser of $5,000, or the student’s cost of attendance (as defined in section 1087 of this title).ll

children.heading: Amounts for scholarships
children.label: 5 Amounts for scholarships
children.text: All of the amounts appropriated to carry out this subsection for a fiscal year shall be used for scholarships awarded under this subsection, except that the nonprofit organization receiving a contract under this subsection may use not more than one percent of such amounts for the administrative costs of the contract.
